summary.intent: Fix issue #788 where Telegram users couldn't use /stop or /peek commands by creating a centralized command registry in moltis-channels and wiring all channels through it.
summary.outcome: Implemented a centralized CommandDef registry with 16 commands, updated all 6 channel crates (Telegram, Discord, Slack, Matrix, Nostr, gateway) to derive behavior from it, fixed pre-existing clippy issues in moltis-node-host and moltis-skills, and merged PR #794 after addressing review comments.

repo: moltis-channels is the shared dependency used by all channel crates; centralized logic belongs here
Nostr command interception was entirely missing before this change; WhatsApp already routes all /commands to gateway
The /sh command requires special handling: only toggle sub-commands (empty, on, off, exit, status) should be intercepted, not arbitrary shell input
moltis-gateway already depended on moltis-channels, so no new Cargo.toml changes were needed for the gateway test
Matrix was intercepting ALL /-prefixed messages without calling is_channel_command(), causing /sh ls -la and unknown commands to route incorrectly
moltis-skills and moltis-node-host had pre-existing clippy failures on main unrelated to the feature work

code.finding: New centralized registry: CommandDef struct, all_commands() returning 16 static entries, is_channel_command() with /sh special-case logic, help_text() generator. 7 unit tests included.
Nostr had zero command interception prior to this change; pattern to follow was WhatsApp handler at crates/whatsapp/src/handlers.rs:396-424
Matrix was routing all /-prefixed messages to gateway unconditionally; fix was to gate on is_channel_command() first
Tests imported HELP_TEXT constant which was removed; needed updating to use help_text() function call instead
Discord command name validation test used [a-z_] but correct pattern is [a-z0-9_-]; also had hardcoded count of 8 commands that needed updating to use registry length
Added bidirectional consistency test: asserts every command in all_commands() (except 'help') has a matching dispatch arm, catching future registry/dispatch drift at test time

workflow: Pre-existing lint failures in unrelated crates blocked `just lint`; needed to identify and fix them to get a clean CI before pushing
Running clippy scoped to only affected crates (--package flags) is a useful workaround when unrelated crates have pre-existing failures
Greptile caught a real behavioral regression (Matrix routing) that tests didn't catch because the bug was in routing logic, not output correctness

friction: Pre-existing clippy failures in moltis-node-host (22 large Err-variant warnings) and moltis-skills (needless returns) blocked the lint check and required fixing before push
Matrix tests imported the now-removed HELP_TEXT constant, causing a compile break discovered only after the initial commit attempt
Discord test had an overly restrictive regex [a-z_] that conflicted with the correct [a-z0-9_-] pattern, caught by PR review
open_items: Discord argument registration for commands like /approve N is out of scope — Discord slash commands don't support freeform args by default, these work via text-based / input
Centralized /help handler in gateway (channels currently handle help locally) was explicitly deferred
Fallback models and markdown editing from web UI (separate features also referenced in #788 context) are out of scope
